title: WebSocket Frame
description: Schema describing the structure of a WebSocket data frame as defined in RFC 6455 Section 5.2. Each frame consists of header fields and a payload.

fin.description: Indicates whether this is the final fragment in a message. The first fragment may also be the final fragment.

rsv1.description: Reserved bit 1. Must be 0 unless an extension defines a meaning for non-zero values (e.g., permessage-deflate uses RSV1).

rsv2.description: Reserved bit 2. Must be 0 unless an extension defines a meaning for non-zero values.

rsv3.description: Reserved bit 3. Must be 0 unless an extension defines a meaning for non-zero values.

opcode.description: Defines the interpretation of the payload data. Opcodes 0x0-0x2 are for data frames, 0x8-0xA are for control frames.

opcodeDescription.description: Human-readable description of the opcode value.

masked.description: Indicates whether the payload data is masked. All frames sent from client to server must be masked. Frames from server to client must not be masked.

maskingKey.description: A 32-bit value used to mask the payload data. Present only when masked is true. Represented as 4 bytes.

payloadLength.description: The length of the payload data in bytes. For control frames, this must not exceed 125.

payloadData.description: The application data carried by the frame. For text frames, this is UTF-8 encoded text. For binary frames, this is raw binary data.
